sources: Traveler forum consensus 2025 (Reddit r/solotravel, Rick Steves forums)
methodology: Risk score 1-5 based on reported pickpocket incidents relative to tourist density. This is not a measure of violent crime, overall safety, or any characteristic of residents. Scores reflect opportunistic theft patterns reported by travelers and local police. Intended to encourage awareness and preparation, not alarm or avoidance.
disclaimer: Pickpocketing is an opportunistic crime that affects visibly-tourist travelers in any busy city. Lower scores do not mean zero risk; higher scores do not mean danger. Basic awareness (bag in front, phone out of back pocket) is sufficient almost everywhere.

hotspots: Metro L3 between Passeig de Gràcia and Liceu
tips: Keep wallet and phone in a front pocket
Beware of petition, flower, and 'found ring' scams on La Rambla
Use an anti-theft crossbody bag on the metro
Never leave bags under café tables
barcelona.note: Barcelona has one of the highest reported pickpocket rates in Europe; violent crime against tourists is low.

tips: Bus 64 is notorious — keep hands on zippers
Watch for groups of distracting children near Termini
Zip bags and carry them across the body
Avoid street 'gladiators' asking for money after photos
rome.note: Rome's metro and tourist buses are the main hotspots. Gelato queues and fountain crowds follow.

tips: Keep phones out of back pockets on the métro
Refuse the 'friendship bracelet' scam at Sacré-Cœur
Ignore 'did you drop this ring?' scam along the Seine
Use an anti-theft bag in crowded stations
paris.note: Métro interchanges and the Eiffel Tower area see the most incidents. Louvre and Versailles are also active.

tips: Watch for bumping tactics on Charles Bridge
Avoid unofficial 'tourist taxis' at the main station
Be wary of currency-exchange scams offering great rates
Keep valuables zipped on Tram 22 to the castle
prague.note: Prague pickpockets work in organised teams on tourist trams and bridges.

tips: Keep hands on bag in El Rastro crowds
Avoid the 'paint on your jacket' distraction scam near Sol
Watch for moped snatchers on Gran Vía with phones or handbags
Use the metro's front cars, which are less crowded
madrid.note: Madrid is lower-risk than Barcelona but Sol, Gran Vía, and El Rastro remain active.

tips: Central station has persistent luggage-theft reports — never look away
Duomo 'bracelet men' will insist on tying one on your wrist — walk past firmly
Metro rush hour is prime pickpocket time
milan.note: Milan's pickpocket teams often cluster around Centrale and the Duomo metro.

tips: Circumvesuviana is the single highest-reported route in southern Italy — keep bags in sight
Avoid displaying cameras and phones around Garibaldi
Moped bag-snatching exists — carry bags on the wall side of the street
naples.note: Naples has higher petty crime than other Italian cities but retains huge cultural rewards for prepared travelers.

tips: Watch for bumping on Ponte Vecchio
Keep bags zipped around the Duomo crowds
Station luggage storage is safer than leaving bags on the platform
florence.note: Florence's compact tourist core concentrates both visitors and pickpockets.

tips: Trams 2 and 5 are the classic pickpocket routes — keep bag in front
Be cautious around Centraal Station platforms at rush hour
Bike theft is common — use two locks
amsterdam.note: Amsterdam's tram network and Centraal Station are the main hotspots.

tips: Metro Line 1 and the airport line have heavy pickpocketing
Omonoia at night — stay alert
Keep valuables off the café table
athens.note: Athens metro is the main risk zone; tourist sites like the Acropolis are comparatively safe.

tips: Watch bags on U8 late at night
Keep phone secure at Alexanderplatz crowds
Club queues in Friedrichshain are generally fine but stay aware
berlin.note: Berlin is relatively safe; incidents cluster on a few U-Bahn lines and in transit hubs.

tips: Standard pickpocket awareness is enough
Watch for 'fake concert ticket' sellers near the Staatsoper
Crowded events at Prater increase risk
vienna.note: Vienna is one of Europe's safer capitals; most issues are in transit hubs.

tips: Ruin bars occasionally see card-skimming — use cash or tap-to-pay
Watch for overpriced drink scams near Váci utca
Metro M3 was renovated but remains a pickpocket hotspot
budapest.note: Budapest's biggest risk is scams rather than skilled pickpockets.

tips: Tram 28 is famous for pickpockets — stand near the driver
Keep phone secure while taking cobblestone photos
Avoid drug sellers in Baixa who may be distracting accomplices
lisbon.note: Lisbon's iconic Tram 28 is the single biggest pickpocket magnet in Portugal.

tips: Watch bags in the metro to the airport
Ribeira restaurants — keep bags off chair backs
Drug offers in the old town are often distraction setups — say no and walk on
porto.note: Porto is generally relaxed; watch belongings in the two main transit routes.

tips: Midi/Zuid is the highest-risk station in Belgium — never leave luggage unattended
Keep valuables in front on crowded Line 1
Avoid Gare du Nord surroundings late at night
brussels.note: Brussels' railway stations are the main concern; the EU quarter and central tourist area are quieter.

tips: Central station luggage theft is the main issue
Keep tram tickets punched to avoid fake-inspector scams
Watch for unmarked taxis charging inflated fares
warsaw.note: Warsaw is safer than its reputation suggests; risk is concentrated at Centralna.

tips: Main Square pickpockets work the Cloth Hall area
Fake-taxi scams at the station — use MyTaxi or iTaxi
Keep bag in front during Auschwitz bus transfers
krakow.note: Krakow's tourist density has grown sharply; awareness matching Prague recommended.

tips: Watch phone in pub crowds in Temple Bar
O'Connell Street late-night — stay with your group
Luas Red Line is the lower-risk tram
dublin.note: Dublin sees more drunken opportunism than organised pickpocketing.

tips: Oktoberfest is the single biggest spike — phone and wallet in zipped pocket
Hauptbahnhof surroundings late at night — stay aware
Normal awareness on the U-Bahn is enough
munich.note: Munich is very safe except during Oktoberfest.

tips: Standard awareness is sufficient
Langstrasse at 3am — stay with group
Station luggage lockers are safe and worth using
zurich.note: Zurich is consistently among Europe's safest capitals.

tips: Cornavin station sees occasional luggage theft
Paquis is lively but safe with basic awareness
geneva.note: Geneva's only notable hotspot is its main station.

tips: Oslo S has seen a rise in station pickpocketing since 2023
Standard awareness on trams
Grønland at 2am — nothing dramatic, just stay aware
oslo.note: Oslo remains very safe overall; the central station is the only recurring issue.

tips: Strøget during summer peak — phone in front pocket
Bike theft is the number one property crime — always lock both wheel and frame
Station luggage left unattended disappears fast
copenhagen.note: Copenhagen is very safe; bike theft is the real crime to worry about.

tips: T-Centralen sees occasional pickpocketing at peak hours
Gamla Stan during cruise-ship days — bag in front
Drottninggatan late evening — stay aware
stockholm.note: Stockholm is very safe; basic awareness handles nearly everything.

tips: Basic awareness is plenty
Market square — keep bag zipped during peak tourist hours
helsinki.note: Helsinki has among the lowest pickpocket rates in Europe.

tips: Drunken opportunism exists; organised pickpocketing is very rare
Don't leave wallets on bar tops
reykjavik.note: Reykjavík is one of the safest capitals in the world.

tips: Standard awareness in Old Town crowds
Watch for overpriced tourist taxis at the port
tallinn.note: Tallinn is mostly safe; risk spikes during cruise arrivals.

tips: Watch for inflated bar bills in Old Town — some venues are known scams
Standard market-crowd awareness
riga.note: Riga is safer than its reputation suggests; scam bars are the bigger risk.

tips: Cruise days triple the density — keep bags zipped
Bus 1A to Old Town can be crowded
dubrovnik.note: Dubrovnik's compact walls concentrate tourists; pickpocketing remains modest.

tips: Basic awareness is more than enough
ljubljana.note: Ljubljana is one of the safest capitals in the EU.
